question: In the context of neural networks, what is backpropagation?

question: The major difficulty in designing a layered operating system approach is ______.
options: Making sure that each layer hides certain data structures, hardware, and operations from higher-level layers

question: Which of the following best describes the behavior of a CPU-bound program in terms of its use of CPU and I/O resources?
options: CPU-bound programs typically generate long, sustained I/O bursts as they transfer large amounts of data to or from external storage devices or network resources.
CPU-bound programs typically generate short, periodic CPU bursts as they perform small, focused calculations or data processing tasks that are tightly coupled to I/O operations.
CPU-bound programs typically generate short, periodic I/O bursts as they retrieve data from external storage devices or network resources.
CPU-bound programs have long I/O bursts and short CPU bursts, requiring careful management of processing and input/output resources.
CPU-bound programs typically generate long, sustained CPU bursts as they perform complex calculations and data processing tasks.

question: Which of the following scenarios may lead to a page fault?
options: A process requests additional memory from the operating system
A page that is currently in main memory is modified by the process
A process tries to access a page that is not currently in main memory
The operating system performs routine maintenance on the page table

question: What is the purpose of a file allocation table (FAT) in the FAT file system?
options: To store metadata about each file, such as its size and location on disk.
To maintain a map of free and allocated disk space in the file system.
To provide a directory structure for organizing files and directories.
To store a list of bad sectors on the disk.

options: Requirements-design traceability links requirements with specific hardware or software components in the system which is used to implement the requirement.
Requirements-sources traceability links requirements with the sub-systems where these requirements are implemented.

Suppose you have a JavaFX Scene with dimensions 600 pixels wide and 400 pixels high. Which of the following code snippets correctly creates a vertical line starting from the top of the scene to the bottom, positioned at 1/4th of the scene's width from the left?

Suppose you are developing a social networking application where Users can have Followers, Followers can React to Posts, Posts can have Comments, and Users can also Comment on Posts. Which of the following Java class structures best represents this design?
options: class User { private List<Follower> followers; private List<Post> posts; } class Follower { private User user; private List<PostReaction> postReactions; } class Post { private List<Comment> comments; private List<PostReaction> postReactions; } class Comment { private User user; private Post post; } class PostReaction { private Follower follower; private Post post; }

class User { private List<Follower> followers; private List<Post> posts; } class Follower { private User user; private List<Reaction> reactions; } class Post { private List<Comment> comments; private List<Reaction> reactions; } class Comment { private User user; private Post post; } class Reaction { private Follower follower; private Post post; }

class User { private List<Follower> followers; private List<Post> posts; } class Follower { private User user; private List<Post> reactions; } class Post { private List<Comment> comments; private List<Follower> reactions; } class Comment { private User user; private Post post; }

class User { private List<Follower> followers; private List<Post> posts; } class Follower { private User user; private List<Reaction> reactions; } class Post { private List<Comment> comments; private List<Reaction> reactions; } class Comment { private User user; private Post post; } class Reaction { private User user; private Post post; }
